title: fraxinus ornus
intro: Fraxinus ornus, also known as the manna ash or flowering ash, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its white or greenish flowers and ash-colored leaves. The bark and leaves of Fraxinus ornus have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus ornus can provide health benefits such as relieving pain, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Fraxinus ornus is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be effectively harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
